name: scale model
description: A single interactive entity bot in a scale model simulation. Holds numeric stats (with start values, deltas, and time units), qualitative state groups, and event listeners. Click to open a menu for setting the model's name, adding or viewing stats/states/listeners, and locking the model. Use this to manually define or inspect individual models within a simulation scene.

name: scale model powerup
description: Generates a behavior-driven simulation (entities with stats, lifecycle states, movement, spawning, per-tick listeners) from a single text prompt — not a literal 3D scene. USAGE: call applyConfiguratorProperties on this tool with { prompt: '<scenario>' } (e.g. 'a lemonade stand over the summer'). A two-pass AI pipeline (planner + builder) does the rest. Do not build the scene manually with makePatch.

name: timeline marker
description: A labeled scrub point in a scale model simulation. Each marker holds a tick value and time unit (minute, hour, day, week, month, quarter, or year). Click to set the value and unit, then lock it — once locked it broadcasts to all models to advance the simulation to that tick. Color indicates whether the current simulation time has reached the marker. Place multiple markers in a scene to define the timeline's key checkpoints.

name: timeline manager
description: The simulation controller for a scale model scene. Tracks the current tick and broadcasts setDelta each step to drive all models and timeline markers. Click to set the time unit, play/pause auto-playback (one tick per second), step forward or backward manually, or reset the simulation back to tick 0 — restoring all model stats and states to their starting values and destroying any spawned bots. One timeline manager is needed per scene.
